items.q: What is the difference between an H pencil and a B pencil?
items.correct: H pencils are harder and make lighter lines; B pencils are softer and darker.
items.misconception: H pencils are darker because H stands for 'heavy'.
items.random_wrong: B pencils only work on card.
items.why: Students guess from the letters; H means hard (light, fine lines) and B means black (soft, dark marks).

items.correct: Slowly burnt wood, such as willow twigs.
items.why: Students assume all black drawing media are graphite, but charcoal is carbonised wood with very different handling.

items.q: Why use a putty rubber rather than a hard eraser with charcoal?
items.correct: It lifts charcoal gently to create highlights without smearing or damaging the paper.
items.misconception: Putty rubbers erase more completely than anything else.
items.random_wrong: Putty rubbers add grey tone to the page.
items.why: Students expect total erasure; a putty rubber is for lifting and lightening, which suits charcoal's dusty surface.
The answer examiners want is: Charcoal. “Graphite” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
The answer examiners want is: Putty rubber. “Water” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many pencil grades from 9H to 9B inclusive?
items.why: Exact answer: 19. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
